published_prior_art.cite: ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004Bounds: armwise missing-outcome selection-sensitivity bounds use the same 2x2 odds-ratio algebra, though not the Lee always-selected alpha profile

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1: ordinal principal-strata LP; pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1: RD manipulation trimming envelope; neither is Lee always-selected alpha coupling

published_prior_art.cite: Lee2009SampleSelection and HuberMellace2015SharpSelection cover monotone/stochastic-dominance selection, not bidirectional principal selection with finite selected-mixture OR and common alpha
Manski1990NonparametricBounds and SmithVanderWeele2019Selection give unrestricted/outer benchmarks, not equality cases for finite selected-mixture collider OR inside Lee principal strata

repo_prior_art.cite: q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1 is only an adjacent estimator-frontier warning, not a partial-ID Lee principal-share threshold

published_prior_art.cite: ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006/JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007/ShepherdGilbertDupont2011 and HonoreHu2020Selection are real comparators, but the proposal does not yet instantiate Delta against their exact published endpoint algorithms

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship equivalence/non-equivalence target is an internally defined 'margin-free armwise beta/psi comparator' and 'Honore-Hu-style' workflow, not a specific published estimator or endpoint algorithm with a quoted claim; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until Delta is instantiated against named workflows.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The beta/psi comparator is formalized through R_arm, but the no-exclusion comparator endpoint is not defined as a mathematical functional, so the claim that the same Delta threshold separates Honore-Hu-style workflows is not yet a closed statement.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the generic margin-free beta/psi comparator with one exact published workflow: specify the Shepherd-Gilbert-Jemiai-Rotnitzky or Shepherd-Gilbert-Dupont sensitivity parameters, its endpoint optimization problem, and prove Delta=0 iff its endpoint law satisfies the common-alpha equations.

Formalize the Honore-Hu comparison as a named endpoint set H_l(P), then restate Conjecture 3 as equality of support functions between B_{Gamma,kappa}(P) and H_l(P) exactly when a principal-strata refinement has Delta_l=0.
Add a computable numerical Delta_l value for the displayed witness and show the actual margin-free endpoint moves when the common-alpha or H<=kappa p0 constraint is removed.

recommended_next_step: Patch Conjecture 3 by instantiating Delta_l against exact published beta/psi and Honore-Hu endpoint functionals; the repo novelty and main Lee-Gamma frontier otherwise survive proposal-level review.
